lavajump.name: Lava Jump
lavajump.blurb: We wanted our kids to learn while staring at a screen, so we built a game where they jump over lava and accidentally learn letters and numbers. It works. They think they're playing.

bookluv.name: BookLuv
bookluv.blurb: We kept finishing books and realizing we missed half the point. So we built a thing that gives you the full picture — videos, timelines, context, podcasts, the “did you know”. Add a book, get the world around it.

reveal.name: Reveal
reveal.blurb: We envied Apple's hello animation and wanted to easily replicate them for any scribble. So we built a thing — pick your layers in Figma, animate them on a timeline, export to video, GIF, or SVG. It's a Figma plugin that makes your vectors move. That's it. That's the whole pitch.

goldengoose.name: Golden Goose
goldengoose.blurb: My wife turned 40 and I needed a gift that said “I pay attention.” So I built her a point-and-click adventure game in the style of classic Sierra — pixel art, ridiculous puzzles, the works. Every room is an inside joke. Every item you pick up references something only she'd get. If you play it and nothing makes sense, that's working as intended. It's not for you. It's for her. Happy birthday, love.
